deep_dive.title: Same Loss, Same Noise, Opposite Schedules: Noise Structure and Optimizer Normalization Jointly Determine Whether Learning-Rate Cooldown Helps
deep_dive.abstract: The cooldown phase of a warmup-stable-decay (WSD) learning-rate schedule, now a default in large-model pretraining, lowers the final training loss in some settings and does nothing in others. We give a provable account of which case obtains, and it turns on two properties together: the structure of the gradient noise and whether the optimizer normalizes its update. On a strongly convex objective with multiplicative (gradient-proportional) noise, stochastic gradient descent contracts geometrically at a constant learning rate, so cooldown has nothing to improve. Under the same objective and noise, sign-based and normalized methods, the standard surrogates for adaptive optimizers, settle on a noise floor of order $η^2$ and reach the minimizer only as the learning rate is driven to zero; any additive noise then reinstates a floor for every method. The mechanism is elementary: an SGD step shrinks in proportion to the gradient and so anneals itself, whereas a normalized step keeps unit scale and cannot. We solve the signSGD stationary law on the quadratic exactly and obtain the floor constant in closed form, prove a local form of the dissociation under $(L_0,L_1)$-smoothness, extend the floor to normalized SGD in dimension d>1 by a scale-invariance argument, and establish robustness to momentum and heavy-tailed noise. Simulation confirms every prediction, and we demonstrate the resulting noise-regime diagnostic on a real classification task with directly measured gradient noise. The mechanism explains whether cooldown helps; the interior cooldown fraction used at scale lies outside stationary landscape-and-noise geometry.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: provable analysis of when learning-rate cooldown helps based on gradient noise and optimizer normalization
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 理论分析学习率冷却何时有效，基于梯度噪声结构和优化器归一化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 理论分析学习率冷却何时有效，基于梯度噪声结构和优化器归一化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 理论分析学习率冷却何时有效，基于梯度噪声结构和优化器归一化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper theoretically proves that the benefit of learning-rate cooldown depends on the gradient noise structure and whether the optimizer normalizes updates, explaining contradictory empirical observations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对WSD学习率调度中冷却阶段有时有效有时无效的困惑，论文给出严格的理论解释：在强凸目标下，若梯度噪声为乘法（梯度比例）噪声，SGD以固定学习率几何收敛，冷却无益；若优化器进行归一化（如Sign-based方法），冷却则有效。该理论统一了矛盾的经验观察，为预训练中的学习率调度设计提供了理论指导。证明了噪声结构与优化器归一化是关键因素。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对WSD学习率调度中冷却阶段有时有效有时无效的困惑，论文给出严格的理论解释：在强凸目标下，若梯度噪声为乘法（梯度比例）噪声，SGD以固定学习率几何收敛，冷却无益；若优化器进行归一化（如Sign-based方法），冷却则有效。该理论统一了矛盾的经验观察，为预训练中的学习率调度设计提供了理论指导。证明了噪声结构与优化器归一化是关键因素。

deep_dive.title: DCVC-MB: Neural B-Frame Video Compression using State Space Models
deep_dive.abstract: In this paper we propose DCVC-Mamba (DCVC-MB), a neural video codec framework for B-frame coding. Our approach incorporates an IBP frame strategy for low-delay B-frame coding, a spatio-temporal fusion model based on state-space models for bidirectional temporal prediction, and an entropy-aware skipping mechanism that selectively omits coding certain latents to reduce entropy coding times. In addition to our model contributions we also implement two inference-time strategies that enhance compression performance. Experimental evaluation shows that DCVC-MB compares favorably to existing NVCs and traditional codecs. The method demonstrates BD-rate reductions of up to $8.98\%$ on average compared to prior neural video codecs, and improvements of up to $30.45\%$ and $1.81\%$ over the VTM-19.0-LDP and VTM-19.0-RA(Inter-GoP=16) benchmarks, respectively, contributing to advances in neural video compression.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: neural B-frame video compression with state space models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用状态空间模型的神经B帧视频压缩

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: DCVC-MB proposes a neural video codec for B-frames using state space models, achieving up to 8.98% BD-rate reduction over prior NVCs and 30.45% over traditional codecs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出DCVC-Mamba（DCVC-MB），一种用于B帧编码的神经视频编码框架。该框架结合了IBP帧策略、基于状态空间模型的时空融合模块以及熵感知跳过机制，以提升压缩性能。实验表明，DCVC-MB相比现有神经视频编码器平均节省8.98%的BD-rate，相比传统编解码器提升高达30.45%。该工作为视频压缩中的高效双向预测提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Benchmarking Edge Inference Strategies for Deep Learning Models in Industrial Machine Vision
quick_skim.abstract: Edge deployment is often the preferred solution for industrial machine vision systems when low latency, data security, or limited connectivity are critical requirements. Several frameworks are available to optimise inference on edge devices; however, relatively few studies have systematically compared their inference-time performance under industrial deployment conditions.   In this work, we present a comparative study of four widely used approaches for machine vision inference in industrial settings: plain PyTorch, ONNX Runtime, OpenVINO, and TensorRT. The evaluation focuses on inference time, covers several CPU- and GPU-based hardware platforms, and includes both conventional convolutional neural networks and a transformer-based vision model. For the hardware platforms and models evaluated, the results show that OpenVINO achieves the lowest inference time on CPUs, while TensorRT achieves the lowest inference time on GPUs. However, TensorRT does not outperform plain PyTorch for the transformer-based model considered in this study.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmarks edge inference strategies for DL models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 对深度学习模型边缘推理策略进行基准测试
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 对深度学习模型边缘推理策略进行基准测试
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 对深度学习模型边缘推理策略进行基准测试
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This study benchmarks four edge inference frameworks (PyTorch, ONNX, OpenVINO, TensorRT) on industrial machine vision tasks, comparing latency across CPUs and GPUs for CNNs and Transformers, providing practical model optimization guidance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对工业机器视觉场景，系统比较了PyTorch、ONNX Runtime、OpenVINO和TensorRT四种边缘推理框架的性能。在多种CPU/GPU平台上测试了CNN和Transformer模型的推理延迟，为实际部署中的模型优化策略选择提供了参考。

quick_skim.title: A Strong Balanced-Softmax Classifier-Retraining Baseline for Long-Tailed Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Long-tailed recognition methods often modify losses, margins, or representations to reduce the dominance of frequent classes. We ask whether, after Balanced Softmax training, the remaining tail error can be reduced by retraining only the classifier. We evaluate BS-cRT, a two-stage procedure that trains a backbone and cosine classifier with Balanced Softmax, freezes the backbone, and updates only the classifier on balanced episodic batches. The second stage keeps the empirical-prior Balanced Softmax objective and uses raw cosine logits at inference. Across CIFAR-100-LT, CIFAR-10-LT, ImageNet-LT, and Places-LT, this classifier-only step consistently improves Few-shot accuracy over the matched Balanced Softmax checkpoint. At imbalance factor 100, Few-shot gains are +5.15 points on CIFAR-100-LT and +5.83 on CIFAR-10-LT; on ImageNet-LT and Places-LT, gains are +6.92 and +9.78 points, respectively, with a Top-1/Few-shot trade-off on ImageNet-LT. We also analyze Counterfactual Boundary Risk Minimization (CBRM), a boundary-probe extension using prototype-based features near decision boundaries. CBRM identifies two failure modes: scaled-logit cosine margins destabilize training, and corrected hardest-negative probes remain head-class anchored. The results support BS-cRT as a practical classifier-side baseline and indicate that boundary supervision must account for class frequency.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 长尾识别的分类器重训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 长尾识别的分类器重训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 长尾识别的分类器重训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: BS-cRT shows that retraining only the classifier after Balanced Softmax training consistently improves few-shot accuracy on long-tailed benchmarks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出BS-cRT，一个两阶段长尾识别方法：先使用平衡Softmax训练骨干和分类器，再冻结骨干，仅在平衡小批次上更新分类器。在CIFAR-100-LT、ImageNet-LT等多个基准上，该方法一致提升了少样本准确率，验证了仅重训练分类器即可有效减少尾部错误。该工作提供了一个简单而强大的基线。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出BS-cRT，一个两阶段长尾识别方法：先使用平衡Softmax训练骨干和分类器，再冻结骨干，仅在平衡小批次上更新分类器。在CIFAR-100-LT、ImageNet-LT等多个基准上，该方法一致提升了少样本准确率，验证了仅重训练分类器即可有效减少尾部错误。该工作提供了一个简单而强大的基线。

quick_skim.title: Backbone-Agnostic Perturbation-Induced Uncertainty Learning for End-to-End Real-World Image Dehazing
quick_skim.abstract: Real-world paired image dehazing remains challenging because haze degradation is spatially non-uniform, illumination-dependent, and physically ambiguous even when haze-free references are available. Existing end-to-end restoration networks usually formulate dehazing as a deterministic mapping from a hazy observation to a clean target, leaving the uncertainty hidden in degraded features, haze priors, and cross-domain negative samples insufficiently explored. In this paper, we propose Backbone-Agnostic Perturbation-Induced Uncertainty Learning (BPUL), a plug-and-play uncertainty learning framework for end-to-end real-world image dehazing. BPUL first introduces a Learnable Perturbation-induced Uncertainty Modulator (LPUM) that estimates channel-wise and spatial-wise feature sensitivity through reparameterized stochastic perturbations. It then develops a Prior-informed Uncertainty-guided Reconstruction Module (PURM), which exploits transmission and atmospheric-light priors to reconstruct the hazy observation from the restored result and enforce degradation consistency. Furthermore, we propose a Dual-space Domain-diversified Distribution-aware Contrastive Loss ($D^3$CL) to regularize both clean restoration and hazy reconstruction spaces with real-world and synthetic negatives. Experiments on five real-world paired benchmarks show that BPUL consistently improves multiple representative backbones. Since only LPUM is retained during inference while PURM and $D^3$CL are used as training-time constraints, BPUL brings substantial restoration gains with only marginal additional inference overhead.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: plug-and-play uncertainty learning framework that is backbone-agnostic for image dehazing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出即插即用且与骨干无关的不确定性学习框架用于图像去雾
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出即插即用且与骨干无关的不确定性学习框架用于图像去雾
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出即插即用且与骨干无关的不确定性学习框架用于图像去雾
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: BPUL introduces a Learnable Perturbation-induced Uncertainty Modulator that works with any backbone to model uncertainty in end-to-end real-world image dehazing, improving robustness.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对真实图像去雾中现有确定性映射忽略不确定性的问题，论文提出BPUL，一种与骨干无关的即插即用不确定性学习框架。核心是可学习的扰动诱导不确定性调制器（LPUM），通过引入扰动来建模特征中的不确定性，提升去雾质量。实验表明BPUL可以嵌入多种骨干网络并稳定提升性能。该框架为图像恢复任务引入不确定性建模提供了通用插件。

quick_skim.title: Geometry-aware Gaussian Prior and Axial Attention for Cervical Cytology Image Classification
quick_skim.abstract: Accurate cervical cytology image classification is a key component of automated cervical cancer screening, where reliable recognition of normal, precancerous, and cancer-associated cellular patterns from Pap smear images can improve screening efficiency and diagnostic consistency. However, this task remains challenging because cervical cells exhibit complex morphology, subtle intra-class variations, and strong inter-class similarities. Existing convolution-based models capture local texture well but have limited ability to model long-range relationships, whereas attention-based models provide broader context but often lack explicit structural guidance. To address these limitations, we propose a geometry-aware classification framework for cervical cancer screening-oriented cytology image analysis, incorporating semantic abstraction and structural priors learned from pre-trained vision-language features. The method uses Gaussian expert modules to generate axis-wise priors from global semantic information, capturing structural regularities such as nuclear alignment and cellular spatial organization. These priors are embedded into an axial self-attention module to modulate similarity computation along horizontal and vertical directions, improving long-range dependency modeling and structure-sensitive feature interaction. Experiments on the Mendeley liquid-based cytology and SIPaKMeD datasets show that the proposed method achieves 99.48% accuracy on the former and 96.08% on the latter, with balanced gains in recall, precision, and overall classification performance. Visual analysis further shows that the learned priors highlight diagnostically relevant cellular regions, demonstrating the potential of the proposed framework as a screening-oriented decision-support tool for cervical cytology.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: geometry-aware Gaussian prior and axial attention for cervical cytology classification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 几何感知高斯先验和轴向注意力用于宫颈细胞学分类
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 几何感知高斯先验和轴向注意力用于宫颈细胞学分类
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 几何感知高斯先验和轴向注意力用于宫颈细胞学分类
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a geometry-aware framework combining Gaussian prior and axial attention for cervical cytology classification, addressing complex morphology and subtle intra-class variations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 宫颈细胞学图像分类中，细胞形态复杂、类内差异细微且类间相似，现有卷积模型缺乏长程关系，注意力模型缺乏结构引导。本文提出几何感知分类框架，融合高斯先验和轴向注意力，利用空间几何信息增强细胞核区域关注，并有效建模长程依赖。在公开数据集上的实验表明，该方法优于现有模型，提升了分类准确性。该工作展示了结合先验知识与注意力机制在医学图像分类中的潜力。

quick_skim.title: Symbiosis-Inspired Knowledge Distillation for Incremental Object Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Incremental object detection (IOD) aims to extend detectors to new categories while retaining previously acquired knowledge. Existing methods often adopt a class incremental learning perspective, separating feature spaces to sharpen decision boundaries. However, this separation-oriented paradigm may overlook object symbiosis in detection, where co-occurrence and occlusion introduce spatial and semantic dependencies that benefit from shared representations. Ignoring these dependencies distorts the shared representations, exacerbates confusion between old and new classes, and accelerates catastrophic forgetting. To address this, we propose Symbiosis-Inspired Knowledge Distillation (SIKD), which explicitly leverages object symbiosis at two complementary levels. Spatial Symbiosis Distillation (SpSD) focuses on symbiotic regions where the old model responds with high overlap to objects in the new task. It preserves generalizable old class cues, suppresses class-specific bias and redundancy, and distills the refined evidence to the new model at matched spatial locations with slot-aligned supervision. Semantic Symbiosis Distillation (SeSD) maintains class level structure by forming confidence weighted prototypes for old classes and aligning their inter class soft ranks over the old class logits, which stabilizes the semantic topology during adaptation. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: knowledge distillation method leveraging object symbiosis for incremental detection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用对象共生关系的知识蒸馏方法用于增量检测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用对象共生关系的知识蒸馏方法用于增量检测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用对象共生关系的知识蒸馏方法用于增量检测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Symbiosis-Inspired Knowledge Distillation (SIKD) leverages object co-occurrence and occlusion to share representations in incremental object detection, reducing catastrophic forgetting.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 增量目标检测中，现有方法分离特征空间导致共享表示扭曲，加剧遗忘。本文提出共生启发的知识蒸馏（SIKD），显式利用目标共生关系（共现和遮挡）在空间和语义上的依赖，通过两个互补分支保留共享表示。在多个增量检测基准上，SIKD显著缓解了灾难性遗忘，并提升了新旧类的检测性能。该方法证明了利用对象间关系进行知识蒸馏的有效性，为增量学习提供了新视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 增量目标检测中，现有方法分离特征空间导致共享表示扭曲，加剧遗忘。本文提出共生启发的知识蒸馏（SIKD），显式利用目标共生关系（共现和遮挡）在空间和语义上的依赖，通过两个互补分支保留共享表示。在多个增量检测基准上，SIKD显著缓解了灾难性遗忘，并提升了新旧类的检测性能。该方法证明了利用对象间关系进行知识蒸馏的有效性，为增量学习提供了新视角。

quick_skim.title: Gradient-Skipping Relevance Propagation for Efficient Explainability of Vision Transformers
quick_skim.abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) are difficult to interpret because current methods of relevance propagation and attention flow do not fully consider some key architectural features, such as the uneven importance of attention heads and residual connections. Prior approaches typically assume uniform importance across attention heads; furthermore, they model skip connections as identity paths, leading to inaccurate relevance attribution. To address these issues, we introduce GradSkip, a novel relevance propagation method for ViTs based on adaptive head weighting and skip-aware propagation. GradSkip models the different importance of the attention heads and dynamically distributes relevance between the attention and residual paths. Experiments on ImageNet1K and BloodMNIST demonstrate a state-of-the-art faithfulness of GradSkip while requiring over 14 times fewer GFLOPs than the best-performing existing approaches. Additional evaluations using transformer-based segmentation confirm improved localization and alignment with ground-truth regions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GradSkip proposes a relevance propagation method for ViTs that adaptively weights attention heads and models skip connections, improving attribution accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对Vision Transformer可解释性不足的问题，本文提出GradSkip方法，通过自适应注意力头加权和跳跃连接感知传播，更准确地分配相关性。实验证明该方法在多个解释性指标上优于现有技术，有助于理解和改进ViT架构。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对Vision Transformer可解释性不足的问题，本文提出GradSkip方法，通过自适应注意力头加权和跳跃连接感知传播，更准确地分配相关性。实验证明该方法在多个解释性指标上优于现有技术，有助于理解和改进ViT架构。

quick_skim.title: GlobalForge: Towards Robust AI-Generated Image Detection
quick_skim.abstract: AI-generated image (AIGI) detectors achieve strong accuracy on clean benchmarks, but their performance drops sharply after images are propagated through real-world channels. We trace this fragility to what these detectors actually learn: they overfit to local artifacts left by generators in small spatial neighborhoods, which are easily destroyed by common propagation degradations such as JPEG compression and blur. Instead, we shift the discriminative cue from fragile local artifacts to more robust global structure. Building on this, we propose GlobalForge, a framework with two complementary modules. The Local Information Bottleneck (LIB) suppresses local components to block shortcut learning, while the Global Structural Reasoning (GSR) module forces every token to gather evidence from distant regions. Both modules are trained jointly under a contrastive structural loss based on degradation that keeps the resulting features stable under degradation. To support fine-grained robustness evaluation, we further introduce RealDeg-Bench, covering 7 common degradation operators and multi-step compound chains. GlobalForge improves average BAcc on 8 in-the-wild benchmark groups by $\mathbf{5.89\%}$ over the previous state-of-the-art, and is clearly ahead of representative baselines on RealDeg-Bench under both single and compound degradations. Code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/GlobalForge-BE0F/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes Local Information Bottleneck and Global Structural Reasoning for robust detection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出局部信息瓶颈和全局结构推理模块，提升检测鲁棒性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出局部信息瓶颈和全局结构推理模块，提升检测鲁棒性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出局部信息瓶颈和全局结构推理模块，提升检测鲁棒性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GlobalForge improves AI-generated image detection robustness by suppressing local artifacts and enforcing global structure reasoning, achieving strong performance under JPEG compression and blur.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对AI生成图像检测在真实世界传播后性能骤降的问题，论文分析了检测器过度依赖局部伪影的脆弱性，提出GlobalForge框架，包含局部信息瓶颈模块抑制短程捷径，以及全局结构推理模块迫使每个token建模全局上下文。实验表明该方法在JPEG压缩、模糊等退化下保持高精度。为视觉检测架构提供了利用全局结构鲁棒性的设计思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对AI生成图像检测在真实世界传播后性能骤降的问题，论文分析了检测器过度依赖局部伪影的脆弱性，提出GlobalForge框架，包含局部信息瓶颈模块抑制短程捷径，以及全局结构推理模块迫使每个token建模全局上下文。实验表明该方法在JPEG压缩、模糊等退化下保持高精度。为视觉检测架构提供了利用全局结构鲁棒性的设计思路。

quick_skim.title: Full-Pipeline Inference Optimization for MiMo-V2.5 Series: Pushing Hybrid SWA Efficiency to the Limit
quick_skim.abstract: We present a full-pipeline inference optimization for the MiMo-V2.5 model family, which combines Hybrid Sliding Window Attention (Hybrid SWA), sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), and multimodal encoders. While Hybrid SWA can ideally reduce both attention compute and KVCache storage significantly compared to Full Attention, realizing these gains in production requires substantial engineering effort. We systematically optimize the KVCache system with layerwise prefetch, SWA-aware prefix cache trees, and specialized placement strategies, achieving strict $O(W)$ SWA storage and high cache hit rates. We further build GCache, a high-performance distributed cache infrastructure with RDMA-optimized networking, and develop a KVCache-affinity router to reduce computation while preserving load balancing. We also optimize for multimodal inputs, including GPU image preprocessing, parallel video decoding, and multimodal cache sharing. Together, these optimizations constitute the first large-scale LLM serving system in production that efficiently covers the Hybrid SWA + MoE + multimodal composite architecture.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: inference optimization for hybrid SWA and MoE models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 混合SWA和MoE模型的推理优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 混合SWA和MoE模型的推理优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 混合SWA和MoE模型的推理优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MiMo-V2.5 series inference optimization with KVCache system and distributed cache infrastructure, achieving O(W) storage and high cache hit rates.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对MiMo-V2.5模型系列提出全流水线推理优化，通过分层预取、SWA感知前缀缓存树和专门放置策略优化KVCache系统，实现严格O(W)存储和高缓存命中率，并构建RDMA优化的分布式缓存基础设施GCache，显著提升混合滑动窗口注意力模型的推理效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对MiMo-V2.5模型系列提出全流水线推理优化，通过分层预取、SWA感知前缀缓存树和专门放置策略优化KVCache系统，实现严格O(W)存储和高缓存命中率，并构建RDMA优化的分布式缓存基础设施GCache，显著提升混合滑动窗口注意力模型的推理效率。
